data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve job processing status and details
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status and details of a transcription or media processing job. Requires job ID and authentication credentials. Returns a JobStatus object containing processing state, progress information, and any errors encountered. Use this operation to monitor job completion and retrieve results once processing is finished.

message: Operation 'createJob' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description is minimal and lacks context about job purpose, authentication requirements, and return value. data.summary_suggestion: Create new transcription or media processing job
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new job for media processing, transcription, or analysis. Requires authentication via API token. Accepts configuration parameters to specify job type and processing options. Returns a JobResponse containing the job ID and initial job status. The returned job ID is required for subsequent operations like adding media or starting processing.
data.reasoning: Original description is minimal and lacks context about job purpose, authentication requirements, and return value. Missing details about what parameters configure the job and how the returned job ID is used. Improved version explains the job creation workflow and provides AI agents with necessary context for job management operations.

message: Operation 'addMediaToJob' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specificity about required parameters (job ID, media source), return value (TaskIdResponse), and side effects (media queuing). Missing context about whether multiple media files are supported. Improved version clarifies the workflow, required inputs, response type, and operational effects for better AI agent interpretability.

data.summary_suggestion: Add media file to existing job
data.description_suggestion: Adds a media file to an existing job for processing. Requires the job ID and media source information (URL or file reference). Accepts parameters to specify media type and source location. Returns a TaskIdResponse with a task ID tracking the media addition operation. The media is queued for processing once added to the job. Multiple media files can be added to a single job.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about required parameters (job ID, media source), return value (TaskIdResponse), and side effects (media queuing). Missing context about whether multiple media files are supported. Improved version clarifies the workflow, required inputs, response type, and operational effects for better AI agent interpretability.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve media intelligence and element data
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves media intelligence data and element list for a completed job. Requires the job ID parameter. Returns a MediaIntelligence object containing extracted metadata, detected elements, and analysis results from the processed media. This operation should be called after job processing is complete to access searchable captions, tags, and media data insights.

data.description_suggestion: Initiates transcription and media processing for a job with added media. Requires the job ID and processing options (language, transcription format, etc.). Accepts multiple parameters to configure processing behavior and output format. Returns a TaskIdResponse with a task ID for tracking the processing operation. The job transitions to processing state and begins asynchronous transcription and analysis. Processing may take time depending on media length and complexity.

data.description_suggestion: Deletes a job and removes all associated media, transcripts, and processing data from the system. Requires the job ID parameter. This operation permanently removes the job record and cannot be undone. Returns a confirmation response indicating successful deletion. All media files and generated transcripts for the job are removed. This operation should be used with caution as deletion is irreversible.
